item.description: Concerns of a return to an all-out war are growing as the United States and Iran trade a new round of attacks in a second day of strikes. It comes as President Donald Trump says he would "bomb the s---- out of them" unless Iran signs a deal. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Fears of All Out War Grow as US-Iran Strikes Continue to Intensify
media:content.media:description: Concerns of a return to an all-out war are growing as the United States and Iran trade a new round of attacks in a second day of strikes. It comes as President Donald Trump says he would "bomb the s---- out of them" unless Iran signs a deal. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

item.title: Fears of All Out War Grow as US-Iran Strikes Continue to Intensify

item.dc:creator: Gordon Lubold
Courtney Kube
Carol E. Lee
item.description: The president is expected to press the leaders to find ways to swiftly increase production of weaponry for the Pentagon, sources told NBC News.

media:content.media:text: Trump Signs Order Directing Customs Crackdown Amid Trade Gaps
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump during an executive order signing in the Oval Office of the White House in Washington, DC, US, on Wednesday, June 3, 2026. Trump signed an executive order designed to tighten customs enforcement, his latest move to ramp up protectionist trade policies.

media:content.media:text: An image U.S. Central Command shared on X alongside a post about the U.S.' completing strikes in response to Iran’s attack on an Apache helicopter.
media:content.media:description: An image U.S. Central Command shared on X alongside a post about the U.S.' completing strikes in response to Iran’s attack on an Apache helicopter.

item.title: Defense industry leaders to meet with Trump amid missile supply worries, sources say

item.dc:creator: Angela Yang
item.description: International tourists traveling through the U.S. ahead of FIFA’s festivities have been documenting their American experiences on social media.

media:content.media:text: Elsa Thora, a Swedish citizen visiting the United States during the World Cup.
media:content.media:description: Elsa Thora, a Swedish citizen, has gone viral on X for her posts about visiting the U.S. for the World Cup.

item.title: They came for the World Cup. They fell in love with Buc-ee’s and ranch dip.

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
Abigail Williams
item.description: Somalia isn’t competing at the World Cup, but from the scenes at Mogadishu’s airport, you’d think the country had won the trophy.

media:content.media:text: Somali international referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan waves to supporters while draped in the Somali flag as he is welcomed ahead of a solidarity football match after returning to Somalia in Mogadishu on June 10, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Somali international referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan waves to supporters while draped in the Somali flag as he is welcomed ahead of a solidarity football match after returning to Somalia in Mogadishu on June 10, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Aymen Hussein of Iraq reacts during a match against Bolivia on March 31, 2026 in Guadalupe, Mexico.
media:content.media:description: Aymen Hussein of Iraq reacts against Bolivia in Guadalupe, Mexico, on March 31.

item.title: Referee barred from entering U.S. for World Cup gets a hero’s welcome in Somalia

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
Rebecca Cohen
item.description: Unite Here Local 11, which represents nearly 2,000 cooks, dishwashers, bartenders and concession workers at SoFi Stadium, voted just last week to authorize a strike.

media:content.media:text: SoFi Stadium workers arrive at union headquarters
media:content.media:description: SoFi Stadium workers arrive at union headquarters in Inglewood, Calif., for a strike vote Thursday.

item.title: Workers at L.A.-area World Cup stadium reach tentative agreement after authorizing strike

item.dc:creator: Mosheh Gains
Daniel Arkin
item.description: Virginia emergency crews deployed to the Pentagon on Thursday morning for what authorities described as a “hazardous materials incident.”Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell confirmed that “sophisticated systems” had “detected an air quality issue necessitating precautionary measures until we determine its significance.”“The Department is executing standard protection protocols, including a shelter-in-place order for the affected area,” Parnell added, referring to the Defense Departme

media:content.media:text: The Pentagon in Arlington, Virginia.
media:content.media:description: The Pentagon in Arlington, Va., in 2025.

item.title: Hazmat crews respond to ‘hazardous materials incident’ at the Pentagon

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: The New York Knicks overcame a 29-point deficit against the San Antonio Spurs in Game 4 to take a 3-1 lead in the NBA Finals. Here’s how it happened.

media:content.media:text: 2026 NBA Finals - San Antonio Spurs v New York Knicks
media:content.media:description: OG Anunoby #8 of the New York Knicks shoots the winning shot during the game against the San Antonio Spurs during Game 4 of the NBA Finals on Wednesday, at Madison Square Garden in New York City.

media:content.media:text: 2026 NBA Finals - Game Four
media:content.media:description: OG Anunoby #8 of the New York Knicks shoots the winning shot during the game against the San Antonio Spurs during Game 4 of the NBA Finals on Wednesday, at Madison Square Garden in New York City.

media:content.media:text: New York Knicks star Karl-Anthony Towns celebrates after his team's 107-106 victory against the San Antonio Spurs in Game 4 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden.
media:content.media:description: OG Anunoby #8 of the New York Knicks shoots the winning shot during the game against the San Antonio Spurs during Game 4 of the NBA Finals on Wednesday, at Madison Square Garden in New York City.

item.title: How the Knicks pulled off the greatest comeback in NBA Finals history to move one win from glory

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: Wholesale inflation surged to its highest level since late 2022, as fuel prices pushed higher by the war with Iran ripple across the economy.

media:content.media:text: CORRECTION / US-POLITICS-TRUMP
media:content.media:description: President Donald at John F. Kennedy International Airport, in New York City, on Tuesday.

item.title: The inflation surge is hitting businesses, too

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
item.description: LONDON — The condemnation over anti-immigrant riots in Northern Ireland was being matched by another growing outrage in Britain on Thursday: that the world’s richest person was inciting the violence

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-BRITAIN-NIRELAND-POLICE-INVESTIGATION-IMMIGRATION
media:content.media:description: Fires burn around police vehicles as protesters (behind) stand off with police in Glengormley, north of Belfast, Northern Ireland, on June 10, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Britain-Northern-Ireland-Stabbing
media:content.media:description: Vehicles set on fire by protesters burn on Lendrick Street in Belfast on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Tommy Robinson speaks at a flash protest outside Southampton
media:content.media:description: Tommy Robinson speaks at a protest outside Southampton Central Police Station in southern England on June 2.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-BRITAIN-NIRELAND-POLICE-INVESTIGATION-IMMIGRATION
media:content.media:description: People stand next to charred cars and homes after demonstrations turned violent the night before, in Belfast, on Wednesday.

media:content.media:text: Elon Musk attends the U.S.-Saudi Investment Forum in Washington on Nov. 19, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Elon Musk has been accused of inciting violence

item.title: Elon Musk under fire for stoking anti-immigrant riots in Belfast

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.description: Virginia emergency crews deployed to the Pentagon on Thursday morning for what authorities described as a “hazardous materials incident.”Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell confirmed that “sophisticated systems” had “detected an air quality issue necessitating precautionary measures until we determine its significance.”“The Department is executing standard protection protocols, including a shelter-in-place order for the affected area,” Parnell added, referring to the Defense Departme

item.title: Hazmat crews respond to ‘hazardous materials incident’ at the Pentagon

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Cohen
item.description: A New York judge ruled Thursday that congressional hopeful Brad Lander is not guilty of misdemeanor charges against him related to a September inspection attempt at an immigration holding facility in Manhattan.“I feel genuinely moved today by the rule of law — by the excellent legal counsel I had, by the thoughtful consideration by the judge, and by the simple knowledge that I could enter and leave the court safely,” Lander, a close political ally of Mayor Zohran Mamdani, said at a news co

media:content.media:text: US Congressional Candidate Brad Lander Court Hearing Over ICE Charges
media:content.media:description: Brad Lander, former New York City comptroller, arrives at federal court in New York on Wednesday.

item.title: Brad Lander found not guilty of charges in incident at NYC immigration detention center

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Dangerous heat and humidity are bearing down on the Northeast on Thursday, following a surge of more than 350 storms on Wednesday — one of the stormiest days of 2026 so far

media:content.media:text: Michigan Extreme Weather
media:content.media:description: Storm damage in Freeland, Mich., on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: chairs flying into oncoming traffic during windy day in Chicago
media:content.media:description: Storm damage in Freeland, Mich., on Tuesday.

item.title: Severe thunderstorms and humid weather rip through the Northeast and Midwest

item.dc:creator: Chloe Atkins

item.description: Vance Boelter, the Minnesota man accused of impersonating a law enforcement officer and fatally shooting the state’s former house speaker in what authorities have described as a politically motivated assassination, plead guilty Thursday

media:content.media:text: A photograph of Melissa Hortman and her husband amongst flowers
media:content.media:description: A photograph of Sen. Melissa Hortman, D-Minn., and her husband at memorial outside the Minnesota State Capitol.

media:content.media:text: Vance Boelter is arrested
media:content.media:description: A photograph of Sen. Melissa Hortman, D-Minn., and her husband at memorial outside the Minnesota State Capitol.

item.title: Man accused of killing Minnesota lawmaker and her husband accepts guilty plea

item.dc:creator: Phil Helsel
item.description: The UFC matches to be held on the lawn of the White House on President Donald Trump’s 80th birthday are sure to be a spectacle unseen in the history of the storied complex

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: With the U.S. hosting men’s World Cup games for the first time since 1994, the attention is on whether the U.S. can go far under coach Mauricio Pochettino.

media:content.media:text: United States Training & Media Availability - FIFA World Cup 2026
media:content.media:description: Tim Ream #13 and Sebastian Berhalter #14 participate during a training session ahead of the 2026 World Cup at the Great Park Sports Complex on June 9, 2026 in Irvine, Calif.

media:content.media:text: United States Training & Media Availability - FIFA World Cup 2026
media:content.media:description: Chris Brady, Gio Reyna, Antonee Robinson and Joe Scally at Community Day in Irvine on Tuesday,

media:content.media:description: U.S. coach Mauricio Pochettino at a training session Wednesday.

item.title: As World Cup opener nears, U.S. men know they’ll play under a spotlight

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: Minutes after the New York Knicks had won Game 4 of the NBA Finals, Taylor Swift led an impromptu pep rally in the bowels of Madison Square Garden, on her way out.

media:content.media:text: 2026 NBA Finals - Game Four
media:content.media:description: Victor Wembanyama looks on during the second-half collapse against the New York Knicks in Game 4 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden on Wednesday.

item.title: Silence fills Spurs locker room after devastating Game 4 meltdown

item.dc:creator: Yuliya Talmazan
item.description: Taylor Swift and Timothée Chalamet were among the celebrities celebrating the New York Knicks’ crucial victory over the San Antonio Spurs in the NBA Finals.

media:content.media:text: 2026 NBA Finals - Game Four
media:content.media:description: (L to R) Ben Stiller, Este Haim, Taylor Swift, and Mariska Hargitay react during the fourth quarter in Game Four of the 2026 NBA Finals between the San Antonio Spurs and the New York Knicks at Madison Square Garden on June 10, 2026 in New York City.

media:content.media:text: 2026 NBA Finals - San Antonio Spurs v New York Knicks
media:content.media:description: Timothee Chalamet celebrates after Game 4 of the NBA Finals on Wednesday.

media:content.media:text: Spike Lee, above, and John Starks, Iman Shumpert, Stephon Marbury, Ben Stiller and Ron Artest, below, celebrate.
media:content.media:description: Spike Lee, above, and John Starks, Iman Shumpert, Stephon Marbury, Ben Stiller and Ron Artest, below, celebrate.

item.title: Taylor Swift, Timothée Chalamet and more celebrities join wild scenes after epic Knicks win

item.description: The World Cup, beginning Thursday afternoon, features the biggest names in global soccer, including two — Lionel Messi of Argentina and Cristiano Ronaldo — who are considered among the greatest to ever step on a field.

media:content.media:text: Lionel Messi wears a pink jersey on the soccer field
media:content.media:description: Lionel Messi of Inter Miami during a match against FC Cincinnati on May 13.

media:content.media:text: Cristiano Ronaldo.
